title: Column types and coercion
text: table derived hb_column_types() drift sync code:
code: library(harbouR) hb_column_types() #> # A tibble: 23 × 3 #>    seatable      r               notes                                    #>    <chr>         <chr>           <chr>                                    #>  1 text          character       free text                                #>  2 long-text     character       markdown blob                            #>  3 email         character       validated as email server-side           #>  4 url           character       validated as URL server-side             #>  5 auto-number   character       server-generated identifier              #>  6 number        double          64-bit precision caveat applies          #>  7 rate          integer         0..N stars                               #>  8 checkbox      logical         TRUE/FALSE                               #>  9 date          Date or POSIXct POSIXct when a time component is present #> 10 single-select character       validated against options on write       #> # ℹ 13 more rows

title: Column types and coercion
text: bundled example data exercises every column-type harbouR supports.
code: samples <- hb_example_rows("Samples") samples #> # A tibble: 3 × 8 #>   Name  Concentration Status  Tags   Collected           Collaborators Reports #>   <chr>         <dbl> <chr>   <list> <dttm>              <list>        <list>  #> 1 S-001          12.4 draft   <chr>  2026-04-01 09:00:00 <chr [1]>     <list>  #> 2 S-002           8.1 ready   <chr>  2026-04-03 12:30:00 <chr [2]>     <list>  #> 3 S-003          21   shipped <chr>  2026-04-05 16:15:00 <chr [0]>     <list>  #> # ℹ 1 more variable: `_id` <chr>

title: Column types and coercion
text: text / single-select come back character. number becomes double (caveat: SeaTable’s numeric column 64-bit; large integers may round - precision matters store text). date becomes POSIXct time component Date otherwise; harbouR detects value.
code: str(samples[, c("Name", "Concentration", "Status", "Collected")]) #> tibble [3 × 4] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ Name         : chr [1:3] "S-001" "S-002" "S-003" #>  $ Concentration: num [1:3] 12.4 8.1 21 #>  $ Status       : chr [1:3] "draft" "ready" "shipped" #>  $ Collected    : POSIXct[1:3], format: "2026-04-01 09:00:00" "2026-04-03 12:30:00" ...

title: Column types and coercion
text: multiple-select, collaborator, image file always come back list-columns, even every row one value none. type-stability rule: column R type every row. Flatten tidyr::unnest need long form:
code: samples$Tags #> [[1]] #> [1] "urgent" "blood"  #>  #> [[2]] #> character(0) #>  #> [[3]] #> [1] "plasma" tidyr::unnest(samples[, c("Name", "Tags")], cols = "Tags") #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   Name  Tags   #>   <chr> <chr>  #> 1 S-001 urgent #> 2 S-001 blood  #> 3 S-003 plasma

title: Column types and coercion
text: entry list file objects name, size, type url - ready downloaded via hb_download_file().

title: Column types and coercion
text: formula, auto-number, creator, last-modifier, ctime, mtime link-formula read-SeaTable; harbouR silently drops call hb_update_rows() hb_append_rows(), can round-trip tibble harbouR without writing back computed values accident.

title: Column types and coercion
text: table rows still comes back tibble every column correct type:
code: meta <- hb_example_metadata() cols <- harbouR:::.hb_columns_from_metadata(meta, "Samples") empty <- harbouR:::.hb_rows_to_tibble(list(), cols) empty #> # A tibble: 0 × 8 #> # ℹ 8 variables: Name <chr>, Concentration <dbl>, Status <chr>, Tags <list>, #> #   Collected <dttm>, Collaborators <list>, Reports <list>, _id <chr>

title: Column types and coercion
text: vignette("harbouR") - five-minute end--end story. vignette("explorer-app") - exploring base interactively.

title: The harbouR explorer
text: can try app right now SeaTable account: click Try demo connect screen. app loads example metadata rows saw column-types vignette - including list-columns file column - every panel exercised without network call.

title: The harbouR explorer
text: Connect. Server URL, API token (masked), optional base UUID. values default SEATABLE_SERVER SEATABLE_API_TOKEN. Try demo bypasses entirely. Overview. Value boxes base name, table count total rows; reactable tables small ggplot rows-per-table. Tables. centrepiece. Pick table; app fetches lazily via hb_read_table(), caches , shows typed tibble reactable column-type badges list-column chips. side panel lists every column’s SeaTable type R type maps . Query. SeaTable SQL console, results reactable.

title: The harbouR explorer
text: already harbour_client, hand app skips connect screen:

title: The harbouR explorer
text: Screenshots panels bundled release - taking requires running app, R CMD check deliberately prevents. interactive demo canonical reference.

title: The harbouR explorer
text: vignette("harbouR") - five-minute end--end story. vignette("column-types") - coercion layer detail.

title: Get started with harbouR
text: SeaTable uses three-token model. hand harbouR either API-token (long-lived, per-base, generated SeaTable UI) username+password pair. harbouR exchanges short-lived base-token first time needs , caches , refreshes transparently expires - never see .
code: library(harbouR)  client <- hb_client(   server    = Sys.getenv("SEATABLE_SERVER"),   api_token = Sys.getenv("SEATABLE_API_TOKEN") )

title: Get started with harbouR
text: remaining chunks run offline, bundled example data, can knit vignette without server.
code: library(harbouR)  meta <- hb_example_metadata() meta #>  #> ── <harbour_metadata> ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • base : "harbouR demo base" #> • tables : 2 #>  #> - Samples (7 cols, 0 rows) #> - Patients (4 cols, 0 rows)  tibble::as_tibble(meta) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   name     n_rows n_columns n_views #>   <chr>     <int>     <int>   <int> #> 1 Samples       0         7       1 #> 2 Patients      0         4       1

title: Get started with harbouR
text: live server single call: offline equivalent returns tibble shape, including list-columns multi-select / collaborator / file values:
code: samples <- hb_read_table(client, "Samples") samples <- hb_example_rows("Samples") samples #> # A tibble: 3 × 8 #>   Name  Concentration Status  Tags   Collected           Collaborators Reports #>   <chr>         <dbl> <chr>   <list> <dttm>              <list>        <list>  #> 1 S-001          12.4 draft   <chr>  2026-04-01 09:00:00 <chr [1]>     <list>  #> 2 S-002           8.1 ready   <chr>  2026-04-03 12:30:00 <chr [2]>     <list>  #> 3 S-003          21   shipped <chr>  2026-04-05 16:15:00 <chr [0]>     <list>  #> # ℹ 1 more variable: `_id` <chr>

title: Get started with harbouR
text: samples regular tibble. Everything already know dplyr / ggplot2 / etc. just works:
code: samples[samples$Status == "ready", c("Name", "Concentration", "Collected")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   Name  Concentration Collected           #>   <chr>         <dbl> <dttm>              #> 1 S-002           8.1 2026-04-03 12:30:00

title: Get started with harbouR
text: Appending updating accept tibble whose columns match schema:
code: new_rows <- tibble::tibble(   Name = "S-004",   Concentration = 17.2,   Status = "draft" ) hb_append_rows(client, "Samples", new_rows)  hb_update_rows(   client, "Samples",   tibble::tibble(`_id` = "r0001", Concentration = 99.9) )

what: R Client for SeaTable Collaborative Databases
title: R Client for SeaTable Collaborative Databases
text: harbouR unofficial R client SeaTable REST API. lets authenticate, read, write manage SeaTable bases entirely R, results returned tidy tibbles column-type-aware coercion layer makes spreadsheets feel like data frames. SeaTable trademark SeaTable GmbH; package affiliated endorsed SeaTable GmbH.

title: R Client for SeaTable Collaborative Databases
R Client for SeaTable Collaborative Databases
text: Everything harbouR also works fully offline bundled example data — handy exploring data model connect anything:
code: library(harbouR)  client <- hb_client(   server    = Sys.getenv("SEATABLE_SERVER"),   api_token = Sys.getenv("SEATABLE_API_TOKEN") )  hb_list_tables(client) samples <- hb_read_table(client, "Samples") samples |>   dplyr::filter(.data$Status == "ready") |>   dplyr::arrange(.data$Collected) library(harbouR)  meta <- hb_example_metadata() tibble::as_tibble(meta) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   name     n_rows n_columns n_views #>   <chr>     <int>     <int>   <int> #> 1 Samples       0         7       1 #> 2 Patients      0         4       1  hb_example_rows("Samples") #> # A tibble: 3 × 8 #>   Name  Concentration Status  Tags   Collected           Collaborators Reports #>   <chr>         <dbl> <chr>   <list> <dttm>              <list>        <list>  #> 1 S-001          12.4 draft   <chr>  2026-04-01 09:00:00 <chr [1]>     <list>  #> 2 S-002           8.1 ready   <chr>  2026-04-03 12:30:00 <chr [2]>     <list>  #> 3 S-003          21   shipped <chr>  2026-04-05 16:15:00 <chr [0]>     <list>  #> # ℹ 1 more variable: `_id` <chr>

title: Environment variables read by harbouR — harbouR-env
text: SEATABLE_SERVER — default server URL used hb_client(). SEATABLE_API_TOKEN — default API token used hb_client(). read call time; can change calls without restarting R.

what: harbouR: R Client for SeaTable Collaborative Databases — harbouR-package
title: harbouR: R Client for SeaTable Collaborative Databases — harbouR-package
text: unofficial R client SeaTable REST API. Connect SeaTable server, read write rows tidy tibbles, manage tables/columns/views, upload attach files, explore bases interactively bundled Shiny app.

text: SeaTable trademark SeaTable GmbH; harbouR affiliated endorsed SeaTable GmbH.

title: Scaffolded endpoints (later releases) — harbouR-scaffold
text: functions cover Tier 2 Tier 3 SeaTable endpoints (links, big data, comments, snapshots, bases, import/export, sharing, integrations, admin, team, scheduler). intentionally stubbed 0.1.0: signatures documentation stable, one raises clear yet implemented error callers can discover surface without surprises. filled opportunistically later minor releases.

title: Scaffolded endpoints (later releases) — harbouR-scaffold
code: hb_list_links(...)  hb_create_link(...)  hb_update_link(...)  hb_delete_link(...)  hb_auto_link(...)  hb_bigdata_add_rows(...)  hb_bigdata_move_in(...)  hb_bigdata_move_out(...)  hb_list_comments(...)  hb_get_comment(...)  hb_delete_comment(...)  hb_create_snapshot(...)  hb_list_snapshots(...)  hb_restore_snapshot(...)  hb_list_bases(...)  hb_create_base(...)  hb_update_base(...)  hb_delete_base(...)  hb_base_size(...)  hb_import_base(...)  hb_export_base(...)  hb_import_table(...)  hb_export_table(...)  hb_append_from_file(...)  hb_share_base(...)  hb_share_view(...)  hb_list_shares(...)  hb_list_webhooks(...)  hb_create_webhook(...)  hb_delete_webhook(...)  hb_admin_list_users(...)  hb_admin_list_bases(...)  hb_admin_list_groups(...)  hb_admin_system_info(...)  hb_team_info(...)  hb_team_list_users(...)  hb_team_list_bases(...)  hb_scheduler_list(...)  hb_scheduler_run(...)

title: Attach a file to a cell — hb_attach_file
Create a SeaTable client — hb_client
text: Builds harbour_client object holds server URL, user's credentials lazily-fetched, transparently-refreshed base token. client environment-backed, internal token refreshes mutate cached state place without reassigning variable.

title: Create a SeaTable client — hb_client
code: hb_client(   server = Sys.getenv("SEATABLE_SERVER"),   api_token = Sys.getenv("SEATABLE_API_TOKEN"),   username = NULL,   password = NULL,   base_uuid = NULL,   timeout = 30,   call = rlang::caller_env() )  # S3 method for class 'harbour_client' print(x, ...)

title: Create a SeaTable client — hb_client
text: server SeaTable server URL, e.g. "https://cloud.seatable.io". Defaults SEATABLE_SERVER env var. api_token Long-lived per-base API token. Defaults SEATABLE_API_TOKEN env var. Mutually exclusive username/password. username, password Account credentials. Used acquire account token. Mutually exclusive api_token. base_uuid Optional base UUID hint; usually discovered base-token exchange. timeout Per-request timeout seconds. Default 30. call Internal: error-propagation env. direct use. x harbour_client. ... Unused.

title: Create a SeaTable client — hb_client
text: harbour_client object.

title: Create a SeaTable client — hb_client
text: SeaTable uses three-token model. hb_client() accepts either API token (per-base, long-lived, generated SeaTable UI) username + password (yields account token). -base calls transparently exchange short-lived base token. Defaults server api_token read environment variables SEATABLE_SERVER SEATABLE_API_TOKEN.

title: Create a SeaTable client — hb_client
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() client <- hb_client(   server = "https://cloud.seatable.io",   api_token = Sys.getenv("SEATABLE_API_TOKEN") ) print(client) }

title: Ping the SeaTable server — hb_ping
text: Issues lightweight request server's ping endpoint verify connectivity credentials. Returns TRUE success errors informatively failure.

title: Ping the SeaTable server — hb_ping
text: client harbour_client. call Internal: error-propagation env.

title: Ping the SeaTable server — hb_ping
text: single TRUE success; errors otherwise.

what: Run a SeaTable SQL query — hb_query

Run a SeaTable SQL query — hb_query
text: client harbour_client. sql SeaTable SQL query string. call Internal: error-propagation env.

title: Run a SeaTable SQL query — hb_query
text: tibble. Always tibble, even query returns rows.

title: Run a SeaTable SQL query — hb_query
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() client <- hb_client() hb_query(client, "select * from Samples limit 5") }

what: Read a table as a tibble — hb_read_table
title: Read a table as a tibble — hb_read_table
text: Reads rows table (optionally filtered view) returns typed tibble. Pagination handled internally; returned tibble always table's columns declared order plus _id column.

title: Read a table as a tibble — hb_read_table
code: hb_read_table(   client,   table,   view = NULL,   limit = 1000L,   call = rlang::caller_env() )

title: Read a table as a tibble — hb_read_table
text: client harbour_client. table Name table. view Optional view name. limit Page size paginated fetches. Default 1000. call Internal: error-propagation env.

title: Read a table as a tibble — hb_read_table
text: tibble one row per SeaTable row one column per SeaTable column, plus _id (chr). 0-row tibble returned empty table.

title: Read a table as a tibble — hb_read_table

text: Invisibly returns client.

title: Launch the harbouR explorer — hb_run_explorer
text: Starts bundled Shiny explorer app inspecting SeaTable base interactively. Pass connected harbour_client skip connect screen, run arguments open connect screen — also offers fully offline demo mode powered hb_example_metadata() hb_example_rows().

title: Launch the harbouR explorer — hb_run_explorer
text: client Optional harbour_client. NULL, app opens connect screen. ... Forwarded shiny::runApp(). host Host bind. Default "127.0.0.1". port Port. Default NULL (let Shiny choose).

title: Launch the harbouR explorer — hb_run_explorer
text: Invisible NULL; launches Shiny application.

title: Launch the harbouR explorer — hb_run_explorer
text: Shiny app UI dependencies (shiny, bslib, DT, reactable, ggplot2) Suggests, Imports — core client works headless. Missing dependencies produce single informative error rather stack trace.

title: harbouR (development version)
text: .hb_perform_raw() longer crashes request returns HTTP 404. 404 error message now interpolates request URL via local variable instead calling .hb_safe_url() inline, cli (>= 3.4.0) misread style name beginning dot.

title: harbouR 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Client & auth: hb_client(), hb_ping(), hb_server_info(), print.harbour_client(). Metadata: hb_metadata(), hb_list_tables(), hb_list_collaborators(), print.harbour_metadata(), as_tibble.harbour_metadata(), summary.harbour_metadata(). Rows: hb_read_table(), hb_query(), hb_get_row(), hb_append_rows(), hb_update_rows(), hb_delete_rows(), hb_lock_rows(), hb_unlock_rows(). Tables: hb_create_table(), hb_rename_table(), hb_delete_table(), hb_duplicate_table(). Columns: hb_list_columns(), hb_add_column(), hb_update_column(), hb_delete_column(), hb_add_columns(), hb_add_select_option(), hb_update_select_option(), hb_delete_select_option(). Views: hb_list_views(), hb_get_view(), hb_create_view(), hb_update_view(), hb_delete_view(). Files: hb_upload_file(), hb_attach_file(), hb_download_file(), hb_delete_asset(). Offline example data: hb_example_metadata(), hb_example_rows(). Shiny explorer launcher: hb_run_explorer(). Tiers 2 3 (links, big data, comments, notifications, snapshots, bases, import/export, sharing, integrations, admin, team, scheduler) scaffolded documented stubs land later minor releases.
